explanatory_strength.rationale: The market-structure discount on a levered ETH wrapper is a specific mechanism with filing-backed look-through math, but the dilution-versus-beta path is not yet hard to vary.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: May 31 ETH inventory and share count are primary-verified, while post-May ATM issuance, preferred waterfall, live spot versus marks, and treasury-vehicle comparables remain open.

downside_control.rationale: Price sits below May ETH fair value per share, but unbounded dilution, G&A burn above staking revenue, and an unmodeled preferred reserve leave the low case near $8.50 per share.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Base 6.3% per year is well below a mid-teens hurdle; bull 15.1% requires favorable ETH beta with limited further dilution.

claims.claim: May 31 filing ETH look-through (~$18.76 per share) exceeds price ($15.69), giving a scarce-asset cushion, but that cushion is not a bounded common claim once ATM dilution and preferred seniority are applied.

The eth_trajectory_net_dilution additive row (+$4.50 base) lacks explicit success probability, post-May issuance reconciliation, and arm's-length comparable treasury transactions before incremental ETH beta can be treated as non-overlapping value.
Nine-month general and administrative expense ($335.7M) exceeds staking revenue ($56.9M), so per-share ETH accumulation depends on continued equity issuance rather than self-funded owner economics.

votes.strongest_counter_explanation: The discount to May ETH marks is a rational price for an equity-financed dilution vehicle: management funds token purchases by issuing shares faster than ETH per share compounds, so the scarce asset on balance sheet does not translate into a fixed pro-rata common claim.
votes.most_important_missing_fact: Post-May 2026 ATM share issuance and ETH-per-diluted-share reconciled to BMNP preferred conversion and warrant waterfall.
falsifiers: Reconcile quarter-by-quarter ETH purchases, shares issued, and ETH per diluted share from May 31, 2026 forward.
Model BMNP preferred and warrant claims as explicit per-share deductions before promoting static look-through to a downside floor.
Tie eth_trajectory base case to observed issuance cadence rather than assumed 10% cumulative dilution.
votes.specialist_findings: Scarce-asset read: the filing ETH stack is real and overlap keys are documented, but the wrapper fails durable-payoff tests until dilution pace and senior claims are proven; abstain from approve.

explanatory_strength.rationale: The ETH look-through discount mechanism is specific and filing-backed, but the proposed gap-closer is passive mNAV normalization over seven years rather than a controllable liquidation, sale, or distribution path that Klarman requires to convert appraisal value into shareholder cash.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: May 31, 2026 ETH fair value, share count, warrant liability, and deferred tax are primary-verified in the 10-Q, yet BMNP preferred claims, post-May ATM issuance, and live ETH spot versus May marks remain unresolved for a decision-grade realization audit.

downside_control.rationale: Static ETH fair value per share (~$18.76) sits above price today, but the component low case ($8.50/sh, -46% from $15.69) and unbounded ATM dilution mean per-share asset floor is not structurally bounded despite partial dhando.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Base annualized return 6.3% over seven years is well below the 15% Klarman hurdle; only the bull case (~15.1%) clears it and depends on favorable ETH trajectory plus limited further dilution.

claims.claim: Filing-verified ETH look-through (~$18.76/sh at May 31, 2026) exceeds price ($15.69), but there is no independently evidenced catalyst to convert that asset into per-share cash beyond hoping the mNAV discount narrows.

The eth_trajectory_net_dilution component (+$4.5/sh base) lacks explicit success probability and post-May ATM reconciliation, so incremental asset value is analyst judgment rather than contractually or operationally secured.

Overlap controls in the component schedule are clean (unique keys, no double-count of static ETH with staking yield), but the warrants/tax/preferred reserve row at base zero understates senior claims relative to common look-through.

votes.strongest_counter_explanation: The market may rationally price BMNR as an equity-financed ETH accumulator where ongoing ATM issuance will dilute look-through ETH per share faster than any mNAV discount can close, making the apparent NAV discount a financing-vehicle trap rather than a catalyst-backed margin of safety.
votes.most_important_missing_fact: Quantified post-May 2026 ATM issuance pace and BMNP preferred-stock waterfall per diluted common share, without which per-share asset realization cannot be bounded.
falsifiers: ATM issuance dilutes ETH per share faster than token price rises for two consecutive quarters.
Preferred or warrant conversion materially reduces common look-through below the component low case ($10.50/sh on static ETH look-through).
ETH fair value per share falls below the low case after sustained spot decline and further dilution.
Management announces a dated liquidation, buyback, or distribution program that converts treasury ETH into per-share cash on a defined timeline.
votes.specialist_findings: Component audit: eth_look_through_today is filing-tier unit NAV and appropriately separated from trajectory; eth_trajectory_net_dilution is additive with null success_probability and no comparable realization precedents; staking_and_legacy_cash is immaterial and non-overlapping; warrants_tax_preferred_reserve base zero pending preferred review is the largest senior-claim gap. No comparable peer liquidation or forced-sale evidence is cited. Realization friction is high: ETH is marked fairly on balance sheet but common holders have no contractual path to cash except secondary sale or future corporate action. G&A ($335.7M nine months) exceeds staking revenue ($56.9M), eroding any operating bridge to asset realization. Price trades at ~0.84x May ETH fair value, offering a nominal discount, but Klarman would not treat this as cash-equivalent NAV without a dated catalyst and bounded dilution.

explanatory_strength.rationale: The levered-treasury mechanism (ETH look-through versus ATM dilution and corporate burn) is specific, but the financing-cycle stress path is not separated cleanly from pure ETH beta.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Filings anchor ETH units, share count, cash, and deferred tax, yet BMNP preferred claims, post-May ATM pace, and a full senior-claim waterfall remain unresolved for credit stress.

downside_control.rationale: May 2026 ETH fair value per share (~$18.76) offers a partial floor, but unbounded equity issuance, $287.6M nine-month operating cash use, and G&A far above staking revenue can overwhelm look-through value.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Base annualized return is 6.3% per year, below the 12% credit hurdle; the bull case (~15.1%) requires limited further dilution and ETH re-rating.

claims.claim: BMNR carries no material term debt, but the equity-financing cycle is the dominant fixed-claim risk: shares outstanding rose to 579.7M from 232.4M in one year while nine-month operating cash use was $287.6M against $340.3M cash.

The warrants, deferred tax, and preferred-claim reserve is modeled at zero in base case despite $92.3M deferred tax liability and unresolved BMNP preferred stock; credit stress can absorb ETH look-through before common equity realizes upside.

Component overlap controls appear clean (static ETH look-through separated from trajectory net of dilution), but the eth_trajectory row lacks explicit remaining issuance capital and probability, so the low case ($8.5/sh total, -45.8% to price) is not a fully reconciled financing-cycle floor.

votes.strongest_counter_explanation: Price at $15.69 sits below May 2026 ETH fair value per share (~$18.76); if ATM issuance pauses and ETH appreciates, mNAV could normalize without a refinancing or insolvency event because the company holds $340.3M cash and no operating debt at scale.
votes.most_important_missing_fact: BMNP preferred stock conversion terms and post-May 2026 ATM dilution pace reconciled to net ETH per diluted common share after all senior claims.
falsifiers: Reconcile preferred, warrant, and deferred-tax claims into a per-share waterfall that nets against ETH look-through.
Track two consecutive quarters where ATM issuance dilutes ETH per share faster than token price rises.
Model corporate G&A and operating cash burn against staking inflow under a no-new-equity stress case.
votes.specialist_findings: Credit cross-check: survivability near term looks supported by cash and absent term maturities, but the equity stub is exposed to an open-ended issuance cycle and an under-modeled senior-claim stack; defer until the preferred waterfall and dilution path are evidenced.

pre_mortem.failure_story: We treated BMNR as a discounted ETH wrapper trading below May 2026 token fair value per share, but the equity became a perpetual ATM financing vehicle. Aggressive share issuance diluted ETH per share faster than ETH appreciated, while nine-month G&A of $335.7 million and $287.6 million of operating cash burn consumed staking yield and treasury liquidity. Unmodeled BMNP preferred and warrant senior claims further reduced common look-through. ETH rose in aggregate but per-share economics deteriorated; the stock severely underperformed direct ETH and never closed the mNAV gap.
earliest_warning_signals: Common shares outstanding rose to 579.7 million from 232.4 million year over year while the market kept pricing a structural mNAV discount near 0.84x ETH fair value.
Nine-month cash used in operating activities was $287.6 million and G&A was $335.7 million, far above $56.9 million of staking revenue, signaling corporate burn before any ETH beta.
Base Lawrence IRR was 6.3% per year at $15.69, already below the ~15% accumulate hurdle, implying insufficient compensation for unbounded dilution risk at entry.
ETH per diluted share trajectory was modeled with only 10% cumulative ATM dilution over seven years despite filings documenting repeated ATM and private placements during the treasury pivot.
Preferred stock (BMNP), warrant, and deferred tax claims on treasury economics were flagged [HUMAN REVIEW] but left at zero in the base payoff build.
forensic_checks: Reconcile quarterly ETH units held divided by fully diluted common shares against ETH spot and filing fair-value marks; flag any quarter where dilution exceeds token accumulation.
Trace proceeds from every ATM, private placement, and warrant exercise to ETH purchases versus G&A, advisory fees, and operating burn.
Build a BMNP preferred and warrant conversion waterfall showing common look-through after senior claims, deferred tax liability ($92.3M at May 31, 2026), and strategic advisor warrants.
Net staking and validation revenue against custody, protocol, and corporate costs to test whether operating cash ever covers G&A drag.
Audit custody, validator, and counterparty exposures against 10-K risk factors for impairment or slashing events.
Verify the SOTP build does not double-count static ETH look-through (~$18.76/sh) with the seven-year trajectory net-of-dilution uplift (+$4.5/sh).
Run Tier-1 short activist registry and forensic short web scan; adversarial pass cited no_hit on short_scan_2026-05-28.md only.
unresolved_items: BMNP preferred stock claims and post-May 31, 2026 ATM issuance are not fully modeled in static look-through or the senior-claim reserve.
No preferred and warrant waterfall converts filing-level treasury fair value into per-common-share realizability.
Management alignment, strategic advisor warrant economics, and Tom Lee chairman incentives remain insufficiently tested (management_facts document_count is zero).
Live ETH spot versus May 31, 2026 fair value marks was flagged [HUMAN REVIEW] but not confirmed before sizing.
Tier-1 forensic short scan was batch no_hit only; no local short_reports/ and no targeted activist diligence on a levered crypto treasury name.

responses.question: Post-May 2026 ATM share issuance and ETH-per-diluted-share reconciled to BMNP preferred conversion and warrant waterfall.
responses.answer: The frozen packet anchors May 31, 2026 at 579.7 million common shares and 5,416,945 ETH (~$18.76 ETH fair value per share on $10.86 billion of token marks) but contains no post-May 2026 ATM issuance, share-count update, or ETH inventory reconciliation. Nine-month filing data shows shares outstanding rose from 232.4 million year over year while the company purchased 3,518,278 ETH units, confirming aggressive equity-funded accumulation through the measurement date. BMNP preferred conversion terms and a full preferred-and-warrant waterfall per diluted common share are flagged [HUMAN REVIEW] and not reconciled in the frozen evidence. Post-May forward reconciliation therefore remains open.

responses.question: What per-share waterfall nets preferred, warrant, and deferred-tax claims against ETH look-through?
responses.answer: Filings document warrant liability of $2.1 million and deferred tax liability of $92.3 million at May 31, 2026, which is roughly $0.16 per share on 579.7 million shares before any preferred claim. The component schedule models warrants_tax_preferred_reserve at base zero pending preferred review, and valuation.json explicitly states BMNP preferred stock claims are not fully modeled. No frozen artifact provides preferred conversion terms, liquidation preference, share count, or exercise mechanics needed to convert treasury ETH fair value into bounded common look-through. Partial filing anchors exist for warrant and deferred tax; a decision-grade senior-claim waterfall does not.

responses.question: Does observed issuance cadence support the eth_trajectory base case 10% cumulative seven-year dilution assumption?
responses.answer: Observed filing cadence contradicts the base assumption. Common shares rose from 232.4 million to 579.7 million in roughly nine months, a 149% increase, while the eth_trajectory_net_dilution base row assumes only 10% cumulative ATM dilution over seven years at 8% ETH CAGR. The bear scenario embeds 12% dilution per year. Historical equity issuance therefore far exceeds the base path, and the frozen packet offers no post-May 2026 issuance data to show a slowdown. The 10% seven-year assumption remains analyst judgment rather than filing-evidenced cadence.

responses.question: Under a no-new-equity stress case, does corporate G&A and operating cash burn exceed staking inflow?
responses.answer: Yes on filing arithmetic through May 31, 2026. Nine-month general and administrative expense was $335.7 million against $56.9 million of staking and validation revenue, and net cash used in operating activities was $287.6 million while cash stood at $340.3 million. Annualized, G&A alone exceeds staking revenue by roughly six times, so owner economics depend on continued equity issuance rather than self-funded treasury operations. The frozen packet does not isolate a full no-new-equity liquidity runway including treasury purchase commitments, but the operating burn versus staking gap is decisively negative.

downside_control.rationale: Price still sits below May ETH fair value per share, but unbounded ATM dilution, G&A roughly six times staking revenue, and an unmodeled preferred reserve leave the component low case near $8.50 per share with no contractual floor on common look-through.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Base 6.3% per year remains well below a mid-teens hurdle; the eth_trajectory_net_dilution additive base (+$4.50) lacks filing support after the research loop showed historical issuance far exceeds the assumed cadence.

adversarial_review.strongest_failure_path: We treated BMNR as a discounted ETH wrapper trading below May 2026 token fair value per share, but the equity became a perpetual ATM financing vehicle. Aggressive share issuance diluted ETH per share faster than ETH appreciated, while nine-month G&A of $335.7 million and $287.6 million of operating cash burn consumed staking yield and treasury liquidity. Unmodeled BMNP preferred and warrant senior claims further reduced common look-through. chair_synthesis.primary_method: component unit NAV with filing-verified ETH look-through and additive trajectory net of dilution
chair_synthesis.weighting_rationale: BMNR is routed as scarce_asset_optionality with primary method component_owner_cash_and_unit_nav. The economic claim is a diluted common share of an Ethereum treasury vehicle, not recurring operating owner cash. Filing-verified static ETH look-through (~$18.76 per share at May 31, 2026 on 5,416,945 ETH and 579.7 million shares) is the decision-grade anchor because it is primary-verified, overlap-controlled, and separated from the eth_trajectory_net_dilution row. That component schedule dominates because it matches the levered-treasury payoff lens and maps directly to the 10-Q digital asset table. Marks credit-cycle review is a financing-cycle survivability and dilution stress test ($340.3M cash, no term debt, but 149% nine-month share growth and $287.6M operating cash use); it bounds equity-stub risk but does not replace unit NAV. Klarman asset-realization audit confirms no dated liquidation, buyback, or distribution catalyst, so passive mNAV normalization is corroboration only, not a competing primary value. Comparable-transaction and replacement-cost corroborators are silent: no indexed arm's-length crypto-treasury liquidation precedents appear in the frozen packet. Lawrence yield-curve base return (6.3% per year over seven years) remains the stance gate, not a second asset stack. The chair does not average static look-through ($18.76), the challenged trajectory uplift (+$4.50 base), and the component-low financing stress total ($8.50) into a blended consensus.
agreed_facts: At May 31, 2026, BMNR held 5,416,945 ETH at fair value $10.86 billion plus 203 BTC at $15 million; common shares outstanding were 579.7 million versus 232.4 million at August 31, 2025.
Filing ETH fair value per share was ~$18.76 at May 31, 2026, exceeding the frozen market price of $15.69 (roughly 0.84x mNAV on May marks).
Nine-month general and administrative expense was $335.7 million against $56.9 million of staking and validation revenue; net cash used in operating activities was $287.6 million.
Cash was $340.3 million at May 31, 2026; the company carries no material operating term debt at scale in the frozen filings.

Deferred tax liability was $92.3 million (~$0.16 per share on 579.7 million shares) and warrant liability was $2.1 million; BMNP preferred stock claims are flagged but not fully modeled in the senior-claim reserve row.

Component overlap controls are clean: eth_look_through_today, eth_trajectory_net_dilution, staking_and_legacy_cash, and warrants_tax_preferred_reserve use distinct overlap keys with no double-count of static ETH with trajectory uplift.

Observed issuance cadence (149% share increase in roughly nine months) contradicts the eth_trajectory_net_dilution base assumption of 10% cumulative ATM dilution over seven years; the targeted research loop confirmed this mismatch.
Round-two votes split one watch (hk) and two defer (klarman_asset_value, marks_credit_cycle); no rater recommended add, buy, hold-for-sizing, or pass.
disputed_facts: Whether the ~16% discount to May ETH marks is mispricing or a rational price for an equity-financed dilution vehicle where ATM issuance can outrun token appreciation.
Whether the eth_trajectory_net_dilution base row (+$4.50 per share) belongs in the primary payoff build given filing-contradicted dilution cadence and null success_probability.
BMNP preferred conversion terms, liquidation preference, and post-May 2026 ATM issuance pace needed to bound common look-through after senior claims.
Whether live ETH spot versus May 31, 2026 fair value marks materially changes static look-through before any capital decision.
Whether management alignment, strategic advisor warrant economics, and Tom Lee chairman incentives support a slowdown in dilution (management_facts document_count is zero in the frozen packet).
chair_synthesis.strongest_dissent: Klarman asset-realization and Marks credit-cycle cross-checks: the market may rationally price BMNR as an open-ended equity-financing cycle where management funds ETH purchases by issuing shares faster than ETH per diluted share compounds, making the apparent NAV discount a financing-vehicle trap rather than catalyst-backed margin of safety; near-term cash supports survivability but does not bound dilution or preferred seniority.

dissent_ledger.issue: No dated catalyst converts treasury ETH into per-share cash; post-May ATM and preferred waterfall remain unresolved
Equity-financing cycle dominates: 149% nine-month share growth contradicts base dilution path; senior-claim waterfall incomplete
Scarce ETH stack is real but not a bounded common claim until dilution pace and preferred seniority are proven

value_range_per_share.low_source: component schedule total (ETH mark haircut, trajectory stress, and financing-cycle dilution)
value_range_per_share.base_source: filing-verified ETH fair value per share at May 31, 2026 (10-Q digital asset table; before unmodeled preferred claims)
value_range_per_share.high_source: component schedule base total if eth_trajectory_net_dilution assumptions held (not filing-evidenced after research loop)

expected_return_range_pct.note: Base reflects Lawrence yield-curve stance gate at $15.69. Low bound reflects component-low annualized path and financing-stress framing. High bound requires bull scenario with limited further dilution. Range is not averaged across methods.

entry_range_per_share.condition: No new capital commitment until post-May 2026 ATM issuance is reconciled to ETH per diluted share and a decision-grade BMNP preferred-and-warrant waterfall bounds common look-through. Nominal discount to May ETH marks alone is insufficient entry evidence.

chair_synthesis.recommendation_rationale: Two defer and one watch across three independent groups. The primary component unit-NAV method shows filing-verified static ETH look-through above price, but base annualized return (6.3% per year) sits well below a mid-teens accumulate hurdle, the eth_trajectory additive row is filing-contradicted, G&A runs roughly six times staking revenue, and BMNP preferred plus post-May dilution remain unresolved after one targeted research loop. This is a monitoring stance pending senior-claim reconciliation and dilution cadence evidence, not a sizing or capital decision.

operational_milestones: Reconcile quarter-by-quarter ETH purchases, shares issued, and ETH per diluted share from May 31, 2026 forward
Publish a per-share waterfall netting BMNP preferred, warrant, and deferred-tax claims against ETH look-through
Track ATM, private placement, and warrant exercise proceeds versus ETH accumulation and G&A burn
Compare staking and validation revenue to general and administrative expense and operating cash flow each quarter
Monitor live ETH spot versus May 31, 2026 fair value marks and mNAV ratio
Scan 8-K and shelf filings for dilutive equity raises, preferred conversions, or treasury policy changes
Audit custody, validator, and counterparty exposures against 10-K risk factors
valuation_refresh_triggers: Next 10-Q or 10-K filing with updated ETH inventory and share count
Two consecutive quarters where ATM issuance dilutes ETH per share faster than token price rises
BMNP preferred conversion or material warrant exercise
ETH fair value per share move exceeding 20% from May 31, 2026 marks without corresponding filing update
Management announcement of buyback, distribution, or dated treasury liquidation program
20% price move without corresponding filing update

price_review_thresholds.action: Re-underwrite component-low financing stress if dilution continues; do not add on price alone
Require confirmed dilution slowdown and preferred waterfall before treating mNAV gap as closed
Reassess whether component base trajectory assumptions are filing-supported or fully priced

thesis_break_conditions: ATM issuance dilutes ETH per share faster than token price rises for two consecutive quarters
Preferred or warrant conversion materially reduces common look-through below the component low case ($8.50 total)
ETH fair value per share falls below the low case after sustained spot decline and further dilution
Corporate G&A and operating cash burn exceed staking inflow with no equity issuance for two reporting periods (liquidity stress)
Management abandons ETH treasury strategy or pivots capital away from token accumulation without shareholder distribution

synthesis.strongest_dissent: The discount to May ETH marks is a rational price for an equity-financed dilution vehicle: management funds token purchases by issuing shares faster than ETH per share compounds, so the scarce asset on the balance sheet does not translate into a fixed pro-rata common claim.
unresolved_items: BMNP preferred stock conversion terms and post-May 2026 ATM dilution pace reconciled to net ETH per fully diluted common share after all senior claims.
Post-May 2026 ATM share issuance and ETH-per-diluted-share reconciled to BMNP preferred conversion and warrant waterfall.
Quantified post-May 2026 ATM issuance pace and BMNP preferred-stock waterfall per diluted common share, without which per-share asset realization cannot be bounded.

dissent_ledger.issue: BMNP preferred stock conversion terms and post-May 2026 ATM dilution pace reconciled to net ETH per fully diluted common share after all senior claims.
Post-May 2026 ATM share issuance and ETH-per-diluted-share reconciled to BMNP preferred conversion and warrant waterfall.
Quantified post-May 2026 ATM issuance pace and BMNP preferred-stock waterfall per diluted common share, without which per-share asset realization cannot be bounded.

component_review.decision_rule: Each assigned rater records accept, challenge, or reject with a proposed range and evidence. The aggregator may not silently average disputed ranges.

items.label: ETH treasury fair value per share (May 31, 2026)
mandatory_checks: Is the valuation method appropriate for this economic claim?
Is the security's ownership claim and economic-unit denominator reconciled correctly?
Does the evidence support the low/base/high range and its timing?
Are comparable assets truly like-for-like after production, contract, location, capital, ownership, and realization adjustments?
For an option, are success probability, time to realization, and remaining owner-funded capital explicit?
Is the component additive, or already embedded in another component?
What observation would move the base estimate materially?

items.label: Seven-year ETH price path net of ATM dilution
mandatory_checks: Is the valuation method appropriate for this economic claim?
